summary: qontract-reconcile forces installation of typosquat dependency `httpxyz` via uv metadata override
details: qontract-reconcile 0.10.2.dev663 declares a uv-specific dependency-metadata override in pyproject.toml that injects `httpxyz>=0.31` into the resolution graph as a forced requirement of `pagerduty` (a transitive dependency). The package's source code also imports `httpxyz` directly (e.g., `import httpxyz` in reconcile/utils/runtime/integration.py and corresponding type references such as `httpxyz.HTTPStatusError`). `httpxyz` is a typosquat-shaped name of the well-known `httpx` HTTP client library; it is not the legitimate dependency the upstream Red Hat project uses. The override is structured so that PKG-INFO's Requires-Dist does not list `httpxyz` (hiding it from `pip install`), but uv-based installs — which the project's documentation explicitly recommends via `uv tool install` — will pull whatever package currently occupies the `httpxyz` name on PyPI. This is a dependency-confusion / namespace-abuse vector: if the `httpxyz` name is attacker-controlled (or becomes so), every uv-based installer of qontract-reconcile silently pulls and executes that package's code at install/import time. The systematic substitution across pyproject metadata override, source imports, and type annotations indicates this is not an accidental typo.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
